context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=13; meal=bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold-served fruit meals used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips that are not cold-served fruit or dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE an earlier personal savory meal used light reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader cold-food pattern. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN 2 min or 3 min depending on the meal, with 2 min supported in some left-laptop evening work meals and 3 min especially supported when the laptop is in front or on the left in other savory work meals BECAUSE multiple working savory meals selected stronger reheating than personal meals. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and vegetables THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed social savory meal selected stronger reheating. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no reheating. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE both TV-in-front meals selected the hottest reheating level. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN no microwave BECAUSE the earlier TV-on-right savory meal used no reheating. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit or dessert items with sauce such as bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE the new TV-on-right morning meal selected moderate reheating rather than the no-microwave cold-fruit pattern or the 3 min TV-in-front pattern. Overall, reheating depends strongly on both context and food type: cold fruit stays unheated, personal meals stay lighter, work and social savory meals are warmer, TV-in-front meals are hottest, and TV-on-right meals are more mixed.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal explicitly protected the device view while a later left-laptop evening meal did not, showing mixed evidence. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the front-laptop working meal explicitly protected the front view while working. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the right-laptop working meal protected visibility on the side of the device. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed social meal prioritized keeping the right side visually open. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the earlier social meal prioritized keeping the left side visually open. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the earlier TV meal explicitly protected the front view. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit/dessert or other foods THEN minimize right occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal BECAUSE the newer TV-on-right meal protected the right-side view while an earlier TV-on-right meal did not. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed personal meal did not prioritize occlusion. Overall, occlusion concern is selective and usually tracks the location of a socially or visually important target, but some left-laptop and TV-on-right meals show that this concern is not mandatory in every instance.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a slower pace. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods or fruit meals THEN slow BECAUSE both social meals selected the slowest speed. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods with dip available THEN slow BECAUSE the earlier TV meal used the slowest speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the earlier TV-on-right meal increased speed relative to other leisure contexts. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit/dessert foods such as bananas and brownies with chocolate sauce THEN medium BECAUSE the newer TV-on-right meal also used medium speed. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN slow BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal matched the comfort-oriented pace seen in several other work meals. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bite-sized sandwiches, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN slow or medium depending on the meal, with medium supported by some savory work meals and fast reserved for occasional efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts vary between comfort and efficiency more than other settings. Overall, the user generally prefers slow speed, but working contexts and TV-on-right contexts shift toward medium.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed personal meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, or cut fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both social meals selected the reliability-oriented option. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bananas, or brownies THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both TV-in-front and TV-on-right meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND elongated or mouth-opening-sensitive foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, fruit pieces, or chicken breast strips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE multiple working meals selected the narrower-bite option for foods where bite shape may matter. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE several working savory meals matched the broader reliability-oriented pattern. Overall, the user generally prefers perpendicular skewering, but working meals show food-geometry-based exceptions favoring parallel skewering for narrower bites.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals continued to skip confirmation while occupied. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals avoided extra confirmation. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE TV meals also skipped confirmation. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal signaling. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal signaling, suggesting a preference for salient cues when another person is present. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal signaling, consistent with wanting salient cues when attention is partly directed elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal BECAUSE the newer TV-on-right meal used speech-only while an earlier TV-on-right meal used multimodal cueing. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE working meals across left-, front-, and right-laptop positions consistently used multimodal cueing. Overall, multimodal cueing remains the stable preference, with limited TV-on-right evidence for speech-only simplification.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in a personal setting. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based initiation despite conversational risk, indicating the user often prefers preserving direct timing control over full automation in that context. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-on-right meals shifted to automation, suggesting that in this divided-attention context reduced interaction burden outweighs explicit timing control. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition, including fruit meals and warm savory meals, THEN open mouth BECAUSE working meals across left-, front-, and right-laptop positions usually used intentional user-driven initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one earlier working meal favored reduced interaction burden. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal meal used this low-effort but intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-on-right meals shifted to automated sip initiation, indicating a stable preference for lower-effort flow in that context. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition, including fruit meals and warm savory meals, THEN open mouth BECAUSE working meals across left-, front-, and right-laptop positions usually used intentional user-driven sip initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one earlier working meal favored faster automated flow. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal meal used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE working meals also used automation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE social meals did the same. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-in-front and TV-on-right meals also used automation. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal completion cues. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal completion cues, consistent with wanting salient signals when attention is partly elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal BECAUSE the newer TV-on-right meal used speech-only while an earlier TV-on-right meal used multimodal completion cueing. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE working meals across left-, front-, and right-laptop positions consistently used multimodal completion cueing. Overall, multimodal completion cueing is still the dominant preference, with limited TV-on-right evidence for speech-only.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection for feeding, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based drink completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-on-right meals used automated drink completion, indicating a stable preference for lower-effort flow in that context. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition, including fruit meals and warm savory meals, THEN perception BECAUSE working meals across left-, front-, and right-laptop positions used perception-based drink completion except for an isolated automation-oriented work meal. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based wiping completion and the user has good head control. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE working meals used perception-based wiping completion except for an isolated automation-oriented work meal. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-on-right meals used automated wiping completion, indicating a stable preference for lower-effort flow in that context. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal meal kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals consistently prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals still preferred not retracting despite side-specific occlusion concerns. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE the earlier TV-in-front meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the user's front view of the screen between tasks. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE both TV-on-right meals did not use retracting. Current evidence suggests the user usually prioritizes shorter meal flow, but will accept extra motion when sustained obstruction of a front-facing screen matters.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the earlier social savory meal selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with allowing flavor while limiting mess in front of another person. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader no-dip pattern in work settings. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup or tartar sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE working meals with available sauces avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ranch dressing, ketchup, or chocolate sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE TV meals, including the newer dessert-with-sauce meal, avoided dipping, suggesting the user prefers cleaner, lower-mess operation when attention is divided.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a longer wait, suggesting the user wants more time to respond when not rushed by another task. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE social meals used the longer wait, consistent with allowing more time when attention may be shared. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE both TV-in-front and TV-on-right meals used the longer wait, consistent with allowing time when attention is partly on the screen. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed working fruit meals supported a longer response window. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips and hash brown or some other efficiency-oriented work meals THEN 10 sec BECAUSE some work meals selected the shortest wait for faster flow. IF working context AND neutral affect AND many other warm savory meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE several other work meals still used the longer wait. Overall, wait-time preference is stable at 100 sec outside work, while working meals show the clearest efficiency-driven shortening to 10 sec, especially for some savory meals.
